build.template: Build NeoDOS using neodev: `neodev build --image`. For quick (kernel+bootloader only): `neodev build --quick --image`. Kernel requires nightly Rust: `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly neodev build --quick --image`. Verify with `neodev check-deps`.
build.description: Build bootloader, kernel, user binaries, and disk image

build-lsp.template: Build the NeoDOS LSP server from neodos-dev-server repo: `cd neodos-dev-server && cargo build --release`. The binary will be at neodos-dev-server/target/release/neodos-lsp.

build-neotools.template: Build NeoTools from the NeoTools repo: `cd neotools && cargo build --release`. Binaries: nxeinfo, nxpkg, nxdump at target/release/.

run.template: Launch NeoDOS in QEMU with neodev: `neodev run`. Options: --kvm, --gdb, --storage ahci|ata|nvme|virtio, --net user|tap|bridge, --bdm (persistent OVMF vars), --headless, --serial <file>.
run.description: Run NeoDOS in QEMU with neodev

debug.template: Connect GDB to a running QEMU instance (port :1234) with kernel symbols loaded. Run from neodos/ using `gdb -x .gdbinit`. The debug build is at neodos-kernel/target/x86_64-unknown-none/debug/neodos-kernel.

github.template: Review uncommitted changes in the repo. Check that `docs/` files (SYSCALLS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ARCHITECTURE_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md, IMPROVEMENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, etc.) are up to date. Run `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly cargo build` in neodos-kernel/, then `neodev build --quick`, then `neodev check-deps`. Commit with `git add -A && git commit -m "feat|fix|refactor: ..." && git push`.
github.description: Build, test, commit, and push to GitHub

audit.template:
Perform a complete NeoDOS project audit. Use the MCP tools extensively: run `neodos-mcp_check_consistency targets=all` for invariant validation. Then:
1. Detect dead code: search for unused functions, globals, and modules with `rg "fn .*\{" --include="*.rs"` and cross-reference with grep for callers.
2. Detect duplicate code: look for similar patterns across subsystems (e.g., two cache implementations, two mount managers).
3. Find API inconsistencies: check ObInfoClass/ObSetInfoClass enum completeness vs handler implementation in src/object/types.rs and src/syscall/ob.rs.
4. Review docs against code: check docs/<subsystem>.md for stale file paths, wrong struct names, outdated version numbers.
5. Check for architectural improvements: identify fixed-size arrays, hardcoded limits, tight coupling, missing abstractions.
6. Update docs/IMPROVEMENTS.md with findings as new LOW/MEDIUM items.
Do NOT modify any code — only report findings and update IMPROVEMENTS.md.
audit.description: Full project audit: dead code, duplicates, API consistency, docs, architecture

review.template:
Review the current code changes for NeoDOS. Check:
1. Potential bugs: look for unwrap(), expect(), indexing without bounds check, integer overflow, unsafe without safety comments.
2. Rust errors: run `cargo build` in neodos-kernel/.
3. Architecture compliance: verify new code follows NT-like design (Ob handles, not raw pointers; no Ring 0 shell commands; RAX >= 77 → sys_ob_* for new syscalls).
4. Naming consistency: kebab-case for files/dirs, PascalCase for types/enums/traits, snake_case for fns/vars (AGENTS.md rule 10).
5. Cross-subsystem dependencies: run `neodev check-deps`.
6. Documentation: check if docs/<subsystem>.md needs updating for changed APIs.
Provide a verdict: approve, changes-requested (with file/line refs), or reject (with rule reference).
review.description: Review code changes: bugs, architecture, naming, deps, docs

docs.template:
Synchronize all NeoDOS documentation. For each step, use Read/Edit tools — do NOT run scripts.
1. Scan `docs/` directory: check every .md file exists as referenced from AGENTS.md (the Table of Contents must match actual files).
2. Check `CHANGELOG.md` for any missing entries since last commit (run `git log --oneline -10` to see recent work).
3. Open `docs/IMPROVEMENTS.md` and verify all completed items are properly reflected (move to IMPROVEMENTS_COMPLETED.md if done, update version references).
4. Verify `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` boot phase list matches `src/main.rs`.
5. Verify cross-references between docs are valid (relative .md links resolve).
6. Run `neodos-mcp_check_consistency targets=docs` for automated doc validation.
Do NOT modify code. Only fix documentation.
docs.description: Sync all docs: verify references, update changelog, fix stale content

design.template:
Before implementing a new NeoDOS feature, produce a design document. Follow these steps in order:
1. Research: read `docs/ARCHITECTURAL_VISION.md` and relevant subsystem docs (e.g., docs/objects.md for Ob changes, docs/syscalls.md for new syscalls).
2. Problem analysis: what is the current limitation? Why can't existing abstractions solve it?
3. Solution design: describe the proposed change. Include:
   - New types/structs/enums needed (with ObType numbers if applicable)
   - New syscalls (RAX number ≥ 77, MUST be sys_ob_*)
   - New ObInfoClass/ObSetInfoClass variants
   - New files or modules (path relative to neodos-kernel/src/)
   - Changes to existing files
4. Alternatives: list at least one alternative approach and explain why it was rejected.
5. Affected components: list every subsystem that will change (scheduler, VFS, memory, drivers, etc.).
6. API contract: for each new public function/syscall, specify: args, return values, error codes, preconditions.
7. Test plan: list at least 3 test cases per invariant.
8. Implementation plan: sequential steps with file paths.
Do NOT write implementation code. Only produce the design.
design.description: Design a feature before implementation: analysis, API, affected components, test plan

cleanup.template:
Find and clean up technical debt in the NeoDOS kernel and user code. Use grep and ripgrep extensively.
1. Search for TODO comments: `rg "TODO" --include="*.rs"` — report each with file:line, assess if still relevant.
2. Search for FIXME comments: `rg "FIXME" --include="*.rs"` — report each, prioritize critical ones.
3. Search for HACK comments: `rg "HACK" --include="*.rs"`.
4. Search for commented-out code blocks: `rg "^// " --include="*.rs"` looking for large commented sections (>5 lines).
5. Detect dead functions: for each `pub fn` or `fn`, grep for callers. If none found, check if it's a trait impl or test.
6. Check for obsolete modules: look at mod.rs files, cross-reference with directory listings.
7. Search for duplicate code: e.g., two implementations of the same algorithm (search for "fn.*same_name" across files).
For each finding: remove if clearly unused, add a TODO with issue ref if too risky to remove now.
Run `cargo build` in neodos-kernel/ after any removal to verify nothing breaks.
cleanup.description: Find and remove dead code, old TODOs, FIXMEs, commented code, duplicates

testing.template:
Validate NeoDOS changes with the complete test suite.
1. Build: `RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly cargo build` in neodos-kernel/ — fix any compilation errors first.
2. Run kernel tests: `neodev build --quick --image && neodev test` — all tests must pass.
3. Check dependency rules: `scripts/check_deps.py`.
4. If tests fail: identify the failing test group (printed in output), inspect the test code in src/testing.rs, fix the issue, re-run.
5. Check test coverage: look at the feature being changed — are there existing tests? If the feature lacks tests, propose new test_case! entries in the appropriate module with descriptions.
6. For user-mode changes: build with `neodev build --image` and boot in QEMU to verify.
7. Report: summary of passed tests, any regressions, and test coverage gaps found.
testing.description: Run tests, check coverage, propose new tests for uncovered code

roadmap.template: Sync the NeoDOS roadmap with GitHub Issues. Run `scripts/sync-roadmap.sh check` first to verify the connection, then `scripts/sync-roadmap.sh sync` to sync all labels, milestones, and issues. Alternatively, run individual steps: `scripts/sync-roadmap.sh labels && scripts/sync-roadmap.sh milestones && scripts/sync-roadmap.sh issues`. For changelog generation: `scripts/sync-roadmap.sh changelog`. The local source is `roadmap/improvements.md`; labels in `roadmap/labels.yaml`; milestones in `roadmap/milestones.yaml`.
